{{Quote|Air Marshal Von Asch trained the pilots we're after. He knows how they ''think''.|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="Tales" />}}
[[Air Marshal]] '''Von Asch''' was a renowned [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] tactician and consultant for the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] project during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Asch trained many of the young [[TIE pilot|pilots]] that eventually formed the nucleus of the Empire's [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] force, but the Air Marshal was taken into custody by the fledgling [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] later recruited Asch into his new [[Commando/Legends|commando]] unit, [[Lando's Commandos]], for his experience with TIE interceptors when a [[Eiattu pirates|pirate group]] consisting of former TIE interceptor [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] began attacking New Republic [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]]. During a tense briefing regarding a mission to combat the threat, the Air Marshal was able to extrapolate that the TIE interceptor [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] were most likely based in the [[Abraxas system/Legends|Abraxas system]] by comparing the locales of the attacks with the [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] requirements of the TIE interceptors.

The commandos traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Radix]] located within the [[Star system/Legends|system]] and were soon [[Mission to Radix|ambushed]] by pirate forces when their local pilot, [[Kine (pirate)|Kine]], betrayed them. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Harme Kiela]], leader of the pirates and one of Asch's former students, admitted that their whole operation was orchestrated to bring Asch over to their side as their new leader. The Air Marshal promptly refused, stating that Kiela and the pirates no longer possessed any honor and had disgraced him by resorting to criminal methods. After the fighting erupted once more, Kine attempted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Asch's fellow commando, [[Isolde Siro]], with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], but the Air Marshal took the blow instead, saving Siro's life. Asch later recovered from his severe injury and the mission ended as a success when New Republic reinforcements arrived.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Lieutenant Kiela. This ruse was for ''my'' benefit?|Von Asch|<ref name="Tales" />}}
Von Asch was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Tales" /> who attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Air Marshal]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and was revered as a brilliant [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] tactician. During the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Asch worked as a project consultant for the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] project and began training [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilots]] in the operation of the fightercraft. He took talented, young [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] such as [[Harme Kiela]] and turned them into some of the Empire's most skillful [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfighters]]. Asch's protégés eventually went on to form the backbone of the Empire's collective [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] force. After the Imperial fragmentation following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Asch was taken into custody by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|6|Unusual Suspects}}</ref>

When a [[Eiattu pirates|group]] of former TIE interceptor pilots-turned-[[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] began attacking New Republic [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] recruited Asch and several other individuals of questionable backgrounds into a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] group to repel the threat, dubbing them "[[Lando's Commandos]]." Asch was chosen for his expertise in TIE interceptor tactics and his familiarity with the specific pilots involved in the attacks. He willingly agreed to aid the New Republic in tracking down the pirates, whom he believed no longer possessed Imperial honor.<ref name="Tales" />

[[File:Von_Asch_Death.jpg|thumb|left|Isolde Siro holds a severely wounded Von Asch.]]
During the initial briefing, Asch's reputation caused several other commandos to question the Air Marshal's loyalty. Fellow commando [[Isolde Siro]] argued that Asch should have been locked away in a [[Prison ship/Legends|prison barge]] for the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] he may have caused while in Imperial service, but Calrissian vouched for Asch's importance in the coming operation. The Air Marshal proceeded to brief the rest of the team on the TIE interceptor's capabilities, and argued that the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] most lethal aspect was its pilot. He concluded that the pirate TIE interceptor [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] needed a [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] or a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to [[Fuel/Legends|refuel]] and rearm from, and extrapolated the [[Abraxas system/Legends|Abraxas system]] as the most suitable location for the pirate base using the known locales of their attacks.<ref name="Tales" />

Guided by a local pilot, [[Kine (pirate)|Kine]], Asch and the commandos searched through the Abraxas [[Star system/Legends|system]] in Calrissian's [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and discovered that the pirates were based on the planet [[Radix]]. Upon landing on the planet, the only heat signatures the commandos detected were those of five TIE interceptors patrolling the skies above Radix, far fewer than they had expected, which lead Asch to believe that they had been led into a trap. The team was soon [[Mission to Radix|ambushed]] by hidden pirate forces, prompting Calrissian to order Kine to keep an [[Eye/Legends|eye]] on Asch during the battle. Kine, however, betrayed Calrissian and shot him with his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], taking Siro hostage just as the pirate forces surrounded the commando team. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Kiela, leader of the pirates, explained that the entire operation had been performed to rescue Asch, who he believed was being forced to aid the New Republic against his will.<ref name="Tales" />

When the pirates requested that Asch become their new leader, the Air Marshal refused, claiming that they were mere bandits and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] without honor, and had both disgraced the Empire and degraded all he had taught them. Realizing that he could not turn Asch to his cause, Kiela attempted to kill the Air Marshal, but was thwarted when a wounded Calrissian attacked the pirate with his blaster and called in air support from New Republic forces. In the ensuing chaos, Kine produced a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and attempted to slay the captive Siro, but Asch stepped in front of the commando and took the entirety of the saber attack, saving Siro's life. Though Siro promptly killed Kine, Asch was grievously wounded in the attack and laid in a pool of his own [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. Before succumbing to unconsciousness, Asch told Siro that she was strong in ways others could not perceive and that she had a destiny. The mission ended up a success in the end, and Asch recovered from his injury soon afterward.<ref name="Tales" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You are a ''disgrace''! We are ''warriors'', not ''bandits''! Men of ''honor'', not ''mercenary scum'' like ''him''! You ''degrade'' our ''code''! Degrade ''me''! Degrade ''all'' I have taught you!|Von Asch, to Lieutenant Harme Kiela|<ref name="Tales" />}}
[[File:AschKiela.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Von Asch scolds Kiela.]]
The brown-haired and green-eyed Von Asch held the idea of personal honor in very high regard. He was very proud of the pilots he trained and thought of both them and himself as warriors. Asch had a very low opinion of criminal elements such as pirates and mercenaries and took Kiela's transformation to their way of life very personally. He typically held a calm demeanor, even in the face of criticism by his peers, but he was prone to violent outbursts when a matter of personal honor arose. Upon hearing Kiela's request to make him the new pirate leader, Asch struck the lieutenant across the face and angrily grabbed hold of the man's jowls as he began rebuking his actions. Asch also willingly tried to sacrifice his life for Isolde Siro's, admiring the woman's strength and believing she had a special destiny.<ref name="Tales" />

Asch was very knowledgeable of the inner workings of a TIE interceptor and was thought of as one of the most distinguished starfighter tacticians in the Empire. He believed that the TIE interceptor was an [[Ace/Legends|ace's]] ship and that the fighter's most lethal aspect was its pilot.<ref name="Tales" /> The Air Marshal was renowned for taking some of the best starfighter pilots in the Empire and turning then into exceptional dogfighters.<ref name="Gamer" /> He also had a reputation that was known to many, including several other members of Lando's Commandos, [[Tiatkin|one of whom]] was astonished at learning Asch's identity. After the mission, Calrissian was baffled at the thought that Asch had taken on the role of the "hero" by saving Siro's life, as opposed to betraying the team and joining the pirates.<ref name="Tales" />

==Behind the scenes==
Von Asch was created for the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story "[[Lando's Commandos: On Eagles' Wings]]" by [[Ian Edginton]], published in [[Star Wars Tales 5|''Star Wars Tales'' 5]] in [[2000]].<ref name="Tales" /> Although the stories told in the first twenty issues of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' were deemed to be outside of conventional ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]],<ref name="Tales6">[[Star Wars Tales 6|''Star Wars Tales'' 6]]</ref><ref name="TOS">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/f20041012/index.html|text=''Star Wars Tales'' #21 Preview: "Nomad"}}</ref> both Asch and the events of the comic were canonized in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars Gamer 6|''Star Wars Gamer'' 6]] article ''[[Unusual Suspects]]'' by [[Andrew Hind]], which continuously misspelled Asch's surname as "Tarsh."<ref name="Gamer" />
